title: Living in Boulder Vlog 4/15/18: Free Audio History Books pages

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs from Boulder on April 15, 2018 about his ongoing project to update his website's free audiobook pages covering history, philosophy, and religion. He explains he finished recording page content but realized he forgot to add links to the actual free audio files on Librivox and Kindle, and that some books he listed as free now cost money. He estimates the full update — re-linking, re-recording, and rebuilding the pages — will take a couple more weeks.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: worth finishing and maintaining; frustrated it has taken so long but committed to completing it
old slow computers made website work much harder; modern computers are magical by comparison

stances.topic: millennial perception of his unfinished website
stances.position: they don't understand the context of building on slow 90s/2000s hardware
only includes free ones; books that changed from free to paid are a minor annoyance he has to document

transcript.text: Hello, Living in Boulder V-Log. You're currently 11.02pm on April 15th.
So, I think I did a V-Log just yesterday. I'm in the habit of doing them every day, even though I'm not exercising.
So yesterday I was all about recording all of my pre-audio history pages, but then...
And I finished recording all of them, and I even recorded the video of a lot of America, some of America.
But the problem is, then I realized, oh no, I never put the links to them.
I have all of the audiobooks you can get online, that you can take with you on your ride, that have to do with history, philosophy, and religion.
And so there's two sites that I got them from, Kindle and Livervox.
Livervox, you can put them as mp3s on your mp3 player, and Kindle, you can bring a Kindle with you.
And, yeah, so I realized I needed to link them.
So when you go to my list, if you want to download one for your ride, you just click them from my list to the website.
And because with a lot of the, you kind of have to find them, because they're nestled in with others.
And then, especially with Kindle, there's some Kindle versions that cost money, so you have to know where the free ones are.
And, yeah, so once those links are done, it's going to be, I'll be ready with it.
But the problem is, I made them, I think I collected the books themselves back in 2011.
So, it's been seven years, so they've added a lot.
I only had two books in Euripides, and now there were like 20.
I only had, you know, I had to add Karl Marx, and now I'm doing Emanuel Kant, so that's good.
So, yeah, I'm going to make sure that all the important authors are complete now as I go through.
And then I'll have to rebuild them.
So all of the recording I did, I'll have to delete all those and redo it.
Just pretty much all of them are done.
And also, a lot of the Kindles, they charge money.
One way that I chose which books to take from Kindle is the free ones.
There are some books that are from history that have to do with philosophy, religion, and history from Kindle.
But some of them cost money, so I didn't include those.
But, yeah, the ones that used to be free, now they cost money.
So I have to add how much money they're.
They're only like $1.
But one of them was $4.
So, we'll see how long this goes on for.
I think it's like 1,600 books that I have to do it for.
And, you know, I have to find them.
And when I build the new ones, I have to get all the data for those, you know.
So I have to go and do the word count for them, find out how many pages there are, because I list that on my site.
So, yeah, hopefully I'll be done with adding the links to my free audio history book pages.
And, you know, and then rebuild them, record the audio of them, record the video of them, and upload that to YouTube.
And then that is going to be awesome.
That'll take my...
And I also have a list that has longest to shortest, which I'm looking forward to have with all the, you know, the links connected to it.
So you can choose exactly how long of a book.
That's another way to decide which book you want to listen to for the day.
So that should be another...
Like, to do all of that, that should be another couple of weeks.
So that's my...
You know, that's what I'm working on now.
But once I'm doing that, it's a big section.
That's a big, big accomplishment.
I'm in the stage of my website now where one month will get me finished.
And this is one of the things...
This may be the most extreme example of me almost finishing something and then abandoning it for the longest period of time.
Because as soon as it has the links, it's going to be ready.
So I'm looking forward.
And then I can explain everything, you know.
Yeah, a lot of people, like millennials, might think I'm nuts.
I'm 44 years old and I still haven't finished my website.
But they don't realize when they were little kids, computers were fucking slow.
So what you're looking at is residuals of...
Back in the days when computers were so freaking slow, it made it so you can never finish anything.
So, but yeah, this computer is manageable.
So I'm super psyched.
Yeah, I haven't really been listening to so much stuff.
Living, listening to an astrology stuff.
I'm interested in astrology.
I want to learn that.
I was listening to my own childhood birth chart recordings.
I'll ask my mom if she doesn't mind if I upload that.
And, yeah, so tomorrow I'd like to finish with the links of all of my books by tomorrow.
And then, that might not be possible though because I don't know how many books I have to do.
New ones that they didn't have before.
So, yeah.
But, yeah, you know, I can usually, I'll, I can go less time without losing interest now.
But I also don't have to go as far.
So, yeah, I'm looking forward to another two weeks of this.
To get this squared away and looking really good.
Or weak maybe, I don't know.
So, anyways, that's enough talking.

Mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm-mm
